attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

- handle_new_user trigger uniqueness fix (migration applied to prod)
- Supabase Redirect URLs wildcard added for creviamvp.vercel.app

- Recovery password flow not user-tested (blocked by signup being broken)

name: Remaining Work Before Launch
description: Feature gaps that must be addressed before the 2026-05-04 hard launch
type: project
originSessionId: 5575c1c2-2ae2-40d7-b4ef-59c8f88d8717
---
As of 2026-04-29, these items are not yet done:

1. **SMTP fix** (BLOCKER) — Email/password signup is broken. Supabase free tier rate-limited. Fix: configure Resend SMTP (smtp.resend.com, port 465) in Supabase Auth settings, or temporarily disable email confirmation to unblock testing.

2. **Recovery password E2E test** — Blocked by signup. Once SMTP is fixed, must test full new-device + recovery password flow end-to-end with a real user.

3. **Lead-to-Contract workflow** — Not started.

4. **Server-side feature gating** — Client-side limits exist but no server-side RPC guard. A determined user could bypass client checks. Needs a Postgres RPC or edge function that enforces plan limits before writing.

5. **Bank Integration (USD)** — Not started.

**Why:** Hard launch 2026-05-04. Items 1 and 4 are highest risk; 3 and 5 are product features.

**How to apply:** When the user asks what to work on next, surface SMTP fix first, then server-side gating, then the product features.

The dashboards exist (CreatorDashboard.tsx, BrandDashboard.tsx) but are never imported or routed. - **Severity:** CRITICAL - Dashboard functionality is unreachable. **3. CreviaConnect Page Has Logic Flaw** - **File:** `/src/pages/CreviaConnect.tsx` (lines 18-23) - **Issue:** When `!session` (user not authenticated), the code sets `setIsAuthenticated(true)` and defaults to "creator" type. This is contradictory - unauthenticated users are treated as authenticated. - **Severity:** CRITICAL - Auth state is incorrectly managed; unauthenticated users may be shown authenticated UI. **4. Missing/Incomplete Tab Implementation in CreviaStudio** - **File:** `/src/pages/CreviaStudio.tsx` (lines 44-48) - **Issue:** Studio tabs include "chat" labeled as "Workspaces" and "invoices"/"contracts" are referenced in rendering (lines 168-169) but tabs don't exist in studioTabs array. Only "link", "chat", and "settings" are defined. - **Severity:** CRITICAL - Invoice and Contract tabs exist in code but aren't accessible via UI. --- ### HIGH PRIORITY ISSUES (Bad UX/Broken Flows) **5. TypeScript Type Safety Issues - Widespread `any` Usage** - **Count:** 27 instances of `useState()` across the codebase - **Files:** - `/src/pages/dashboard/CreatorDashboard.tsx` (lines 8-9): `useState(null)` for profile and creatorProfile - `/src/pages/dashboard/BrandDashboard.tsx` (lines 8-9): Same pattern - `/src/pages/CreviaLink.tsx` (lines 49-50): `useState(null)` for profile and linkProfile - `/src/components/navigation/AppLayout.tsx` (line 18): `useState(null)` for profile - Multiple other component files with similar patterns - **Issue:** No type definitions for database models. Makes refactoring risky and prevents IDE autocomplete. - **Severity:** HIGH - Technical debt; future maintenance hazard. **6. Untyped `as any` Casts** - **Files:** - `/src/pages/PublicProfile.tsx` (line 232): `getButtonVariant(button.style) as any` - `/src/components/studio/ContractsTab.tsx`: `setSortBy(v as any)` - `/src/components/studio/SmartInvoicesTab.tsx`: `setSortBy(v as any)` - `/src/components/crevia-connect/shared/CreviaChat.tsx`: Multiple `as any` casts - **Severity:** HIGH - Masks type errors; defeats TypeScript safety. **7. No Loading/Error States on Critical Forms** - **File:** `/src/pages/Auth.tsx` - **Issue:** While Auth form has loading state (`isLoading`), it lacks error boundaries and doesn't show specific error messages for common failures (network timeout, rate limiting). - **Severity:** HIGH - Users won't know why auth fails. **8. Disabled Buttons with No Functionality** - **File:** `/src/pages/profile/PaymentsBilling.tsx` - **Issue:** Contains `` with no explanation or tooltip about why it's disabled. - **Severity:** HIGH - Confusing UX; should show plan details or active state instead. **9. Empty Dashboard Metrics with Hardcoded Zeros** - **Files:** - `/src/pages/dashboard/CreatorDashboard.tsx` (lines 51, 59, 67, 75, 83): All metrics hardcoded to "$0", "0" - `/src/pages/dashboard/BrandDashboard.tsx` (lines 51, 59, 67, 75, 83): Same pattern - **Issue:** No actual data fetching; dashboards are non-functional display shells - **Severity:** HIGH - Users see meaningless data; no actual dashboard functionality. **10. Missing Alt Text on User-Generated Content Images** - **File:** `/src/pages/PublicProfile.tsx` (line 232) - **Issue:** `` - uses profile name as alt, but should describe what the image shows (e.g., "Profile picture of [name]") - **Severity:** HIGH - Accessibility issue for screen readers. **11. CreviaLink Tab Navigation Inconsistency** - **File:** `/src/pages/CreviaLink.tsx` (lines 559-560) - **Issue:** When embedded in Studio, `embeddedTab` is derived from URL `?section` parameter, but in standalone mode it reads from `?tab`. Inconsistent parameter naming. - **Severity:** HIGH - Deep linking breaks in one mode or the other. --- ### MEDIUM PRIORITY ISSUES (Polish/UX Gaps) **12. Inconsistent Spacing Across Pages** - **Files:** Multiple - **Issues:** - `/src/pages/Home.tsx`: Uses `pt-28 md:pt-40 pb-20 md:pb-32` on hero (line 15), but `/src/pages/Pricing.tsx` uses `pt-28 md:pt-36 pb-8 md:pb-12` (line 156) - inconsistent spacing proportions - Dashboard pages use `px-6` and `py-8` (CreatorDashboard line 36) while CreviaLink uses `px-5 sm:px-6 md:px-8` (line 1001) - inconsistent responsive breakpoints - **Severity:** MEDIUM - Visual inconsistency across product. **13. Hard-Coded Dashboard Empty States Without CTAs** - **File:** `/src/pages/dashboard/CreatorDashboard.tsx` (lines 104-117) - **Issue:** Kira Insights section shows "No active opportunities yet", "Complete your profile to see insights", etc. - placeholder text without actual action buttons or links to complete profiles - **Severity:** MEDIUM - Empty states don't guide users; no clear next steps. **16. Button Style Inconsistency Between Pages** - **Issues:** - `/src/pages/Home.tsx` buttons use `bg-bronze hover:bg-bronze-dark text-base md:text-lg px-10 py-7` (line 41) - `/src/pages/Pricing.tsx` buttons use similar but with `size="lg"` (line 320) - `/src/pages/CreviaLink.tsx` buttons use `h-12 md:h-16 text-base md:text-lg` (line 1026) - **Severity:** MEDIUM - Inconsistent visual hierarchy across product. **17.
